rationale: Van Heerde et al. (2013) is cited in the conceptual foundation as direct prior evidence that marketing effectiveness, especially price elasticity, varies with macro conditions. The citing paper uses that point to motivate its own expectation that a soda-tax shock can change effectiveness parameters, not just prices or sales levels. It also explicitly positions its contribution as moving beyond a business-cycle lens (where Van Heerde et al. is a key example) to a policy-shock setting with additional promotion conduct variables. This is more than a passing mention and functions as meaningful intellectual grounding.
relation_description: The citing paper extends the line of work exemplified by Van Heerde et al. (2013) by testing context-dependent marketing effectiveness in a new setting: soda-tax introductions. It broadens the scope from business-cycle effects on price/advertising effectiveness to policy-driven changes in price and multiple retail promotion conduct effects.
